hours_formula_detail: Delaware applies the flat federal ABAWD work standard: at least 80 hours per month (about 20 hours/week). Unpaid community service / volunteering counts toward those 80 hours at actual measured hours — it is NOT an allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula for general volunteering, and qualifying volunteer work is NOT gated on the site being a registered state workfare/SICS provider. DSSM (Delaware Social Services Manual) §9018.2 (codified at 16 Del. Admin. Code §9018.2, tied to 7 CFR 273.24) defines qualifying 'work' as: A. work in exchange for money; B. work in exchange for goods or services (in-kind work); C. unpaid work which is verified; D. any combination of the above. The official DHSS SNAP guidance states the recipient must 'spend at least 80 hours working and/or participate in the SNAP Employment and Training Program each month' and that 'Work can be for pay, for goods or services (for something other than money), unpaid, or as a volunteer.' OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, signed July 2025) did NOT change the 80-hour figure — it expanded the population subject to the requirement (upper age limit raised from 54 to 64 effective Nov 1, 2025; dependent-child exemption tightened from under-18 to under-14) and tightened area-waiver criteria.
minimum_wage_detail: Not used in the hours computation because Delaware uses the flat 80-hour standard, not an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general community service. For context, Delaware's state minimum wage is $15.00/hr (effective Jan 1, 2025, unchanged in 2026, per SB 15 of 2021), well above the federal $7.25 floor; this rate would govern any workfare-style context but the general volunteer lane is hour-based, not wage-divided. Per instructions, minimumWageUsd is set to 0 for a federal_80 classification.
qualifying_org_definition: Any entity for which the ABAWD performs verifiable work. DSSM §9018.2 defines qualifying work as (A) work in exchange for money, (B) work in exchange for goods or services (in-kind work), and (C) unpaid work which is verified — with no requirement that the organization be pre-approved, registered with DSS, hold an MOU, or be a designated workfare/SICS provider. No state-maintained approved volunteer-site list was found in any primary source (E&T State Plan, Administrative Code, or DSSM). Official DHSS guidance corroborates by listing 'unpaid, or as a volunteer' work as qualifying and accepting 'a letter or phone call from your employer or volunteer organization certifying your hours.'
qualifyingOrgShort: Any organization where the recipient performs verifiable unpaid work; DSSM §9018.2 counts 'unpaid work which is verified' with no requirement that the org be pre-approved, registered, hold an MOU, or be a designated workfare/SICS provider.
verification_instrument: No Delaware-specific named form/number for volunteer-work-hour verification was found in any primary source. DSSM §9018.2 states 'Unpaid work which is verified' qualifies; DSSM §9038 lists 'Work hours for individuals who are satisfying the ABAWD work requirements' as a mandatory verification item at recertification. Per DSSM §9034-9035, documentary evidence is the primary method (e.g., a written statement/letter from the organization), and collateral contact (a phone call to the organization) is explicitly acceptable; the household bears primary responsibility for providing verification. Official DHSS SNAP guidance confirms acceptable proof is 'a pay stub, time sheet, or letter or phone call from your employer or volunteer organization certifying your hours.'
accepts_outofstate_detail: DSSM §9018.2 defines qualifying unpaid work as 'unpaid work which is verified' with no requirement that the organization be in-state, pre-approved, or registered with Delaware DSS. The policy is silent on org location. No source found restricting qualifying volunteer organizations to Delaware-incorporated/registered entities. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying organization regardless of state of incorporation satisfies the requirement absent an explicit state restriction — but because no source affirmatively states out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance, recorded as 'silent.'
geographic_waivers: None active in FY2026 — fully enforced statewide. Delaware previously held ABAWD time-limit waivers for Wilmington City, Kent County, and Sussex County through September 30, 2025 (it had adopted broad coverage in FY2024). Those waivers expired at the end of FY2025 and were not renewed: OBBBA (signed July 2025) tightened area-waiver criteria (generally requiring ~10% unemployment or the LMA-based extended-benefits trigger), which Delaware's areas do not meet. The official DHSS SNAP Alert page confirms the expanded ABAWD work requirements took effect November 1, 2025 statewide (a one-month November 2025 administrative pause occurred due to the federal shutdown, not an ABAWD geographic waiver). Statewide enforcement confirmed for FY2026.
geographicWaivers: None active in FY2026. Delaware is enforced statewide. Prior waivers for Wilmington City, Kent County, and Sussex County expired Sept 30, 2025 and were not renewed under OBBBA's tightened (~10% unemployment) waiver criteria. Expanded ABAWD requirements took effect statewide Nov 1, 2025.
submissionMechanism: Recipient reports/verifies work and volunteer hours with their assigned DSS case manager. Primary channel is by phone (DHSS line 1-866-843-7212) or in person at a State Service Center; documentation is a pay stub, time sheet, or a letter/phone call from the employer or volunteer organization certifying hours. No online self-service portal or named paper volunteer-hour form was identified in public sources.
reportingCadence: Monthly. The recipient must satisfy the 80-hour standard each month. If hours drop below the threshold, the recipient should notify the case manager (DHSS instructs recipients who received a notice to call 1-866-843-7212 to verify work/exemption status). Formal verification of ABAWD work hours is also required at recertification per DSSM §9038.
submission_deadline: No specific public-facing paper deadline (e.g., 'by the 10th') was found in any Delaware source. Recipients are directed to contact their case manager to verify compliance; changes that affect ABAWD compliance should be reported promptly (DSSM change-reporting standards apply). Formal work-hour verification is required at recertification (DSSM §9038).
notes:
VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22, FY2026/OBBBA): Confirmed the prior HIGH-confidence finding. Classification is federal_80 — Delaware applies the flat 80-hour/month (20 hr/wk) federal ABAWD standard, and unpaid community service / volunteering counts toward those 80 hours at actual hours (NOT an allotment/minimum-wage workfare formula, and NOT gated on the site being a registered workfare/SICS provider). FY2026 web verification corroborated every key field: (a) 80 hours/month confirmed by the official DHSS SNAP Alert page ('at least 80 hours every month working and/or participating in the SNAP Employment and Training Program'), the Delaware SNAP screener, Ballotpedia, and Propel's 2026 guide; (b) volunteer/unpaid work explicitly counts, per official DHSS guidance: 'Work can be for pay, for goods or services (for something other than money), unpaid, or as a volunteer' — this directly establishes the general volunteer lane; (c) verification is recipient self-report to a DSS case manager with documentation — DHSS states acceptable proof is 'a pay stub, time sheet, or letter or phone call from your employer or volunteer organization certifying your hours,' and directs recipients to call 1-866-843-7212 to verify work/exemption status; no named Delaware volunteer-hour form exists (verification form = unknown); (d) statewide enforcement in FY2026 confirmed — prior waivers for Wilmington City, Kent County, and Sussex County expired Sept 30, 2025 and were not renewed under OBBBA's tightened (~10% unemployment) area-waiver criteria; expanded ABAWD requirements took effect Nov 1, 2025 (a separate one-month November 2025 administrative pause due to the federal shutdown is not an ABAWD geographic waiver); (e) OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, July 2025) did NOT change the 80-hour figure — it raised the upper age limit from 54 to 64 (effective Nov 1, 2025) and tightened the dependent-child exemption from under-18 to under-14. No source addressed remote/virtual volunteering (remote_stance = silent) or out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance (silent). County variation = false (state-administered, uniform policy). Direct WebFetch of the FY2025 E&T State Plan PDF, the USDA FNS DE state-plan page, Ballotpedia, and two USDA FNA waiver pages timed out / returned 429 this pass; their content is corroborated by readable secondary sources and the prior primary-source extractions (see FINAL PASS below). Confidence retained at HIGH: the state's own operational policy (DSSM §9018.2, directly read in the prior FINAL pass) and the live official DHSS SNAP guidance both unambiguously confirm classification + 80 hours + the general volunteer lane, and all FY2026/OBBBA web sources are consistent. The only residual gap is the absence of a published, named verification form — but DHSS explicitly accepts a letter or even a phone call from the organization, so the lack of a form is itself a confirmed policy feature, not a research gap.

--- PRIOR-PASS HISTORY (preserved) ---
FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully downloaded and extracted the full FY2025 Delaware SNAP E&T State Plan PDF (608KB, 91 pages) via the Wayback Machine snapshot at web.archive.org/web/20250830082039, and the full DSSM chapter 9000 (Food Stamp Program) from its 2016 Wayback snapshot. Key findings: (1) DSSM §9018.2 ('Maintaining and Regaining ABAWD Eligibility', 7 CFR 273.24) defines 'work' as: A. work in exchange for money; B. work in exchange for goods or services (in-kind work); C. unpaid work which is verified; D. any combination — the state's own operational definition; unpaid/volunteer work is a qualifying activity with no org pre-approval requirement. (2) To regain eligibility an ABAWD must 'work (paid or non-paid) for 80 hours or more.' (3) DSSM §9038 lists 'Work hours for individuals who are satisfying the ABAWD work requirements' as a mandatory verification item at recertification. (4) DSSM §9034-9035: documentary evidence is primary; collateral contacts (phone calls to an employer/organization) acceptable; households bear primary responsibility; no named form. (5) Del. Admin. Code Title 16 Chapter 10000 (SNAP E&T) confirms E&T is fully voluntary ('Voluntary per 7 CFR 273.7(e)(5)(i)'). (6) Adversarial check: no pre-approved org list, no MOU requirement, no EDP prestep found in any primary source.
EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Hours formula federal_80 confirmed; out-of-state nonprofit silent; geographic waivers expired (Wilmington/Kent/Sussex through Sept 30 2025), statewide enforcement; submission UX = phone to case manager (1-866-843-7212) or in person at State Service Center; reporting monthly; verification at recertification per DSSM §9038; no named verification form.
REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial research attempting to refute the Tier 1 classification — searched for FY2026/FY2027 E&T plan volunteer requirements, work-registration/case-manager prestep, approved-site lists / MOU gating, and verification forms. Found: no pre-approved site list, no mandatory EDP/work-registration prestep, volunteer work confirmed to count by multiple sources, no named form. Tier 1 survives adversarial check.
conflictsWithPrior: submission_mechanism normalized from prior ['paper_county_office','unknown'] to ['phone_case_manager','paper_state_service_center','mail'] — official DHSS guidance directs recipients to phone their case manager (1-866-843-7212) or report in person at a State Service Center; Delaware's prior 'paper_county_office' framing was imprecise (Delaware uses State Service Centers, not county offices), and 'unknown' is resolved.

classification field added explicitly as 'federal_80' (prior file carried hours_formula='federal_80' but did not surface the high-stakes 'classification' field). Affirmatively confirmed Delaware does NOT use an allotment/minimum-wage formula and does NOT gate the general volunteer lane on workfare-provider registration.
unresolved: No named org-facing verification form/number for volunteer/community-service hours exists in Delaware; DHSS explicitly accepts a letter, time sheet, or even a phone call from the organization, reviewed by the DSS case manager. (This is a confirmed policy feature, not merely an unfilled field.)

Remote/virtual volunteering eligibility is not addressed by DSSM §9018.2, the E&T State Plan, the DHSS SNAP Alert page, or any readable secondary source — operationally unknown (recorded as 'silent').
Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is not affirmatively stated; policy is silent on org location and would default to federal rules, but no source confirms.

No specific public-facing reporting deadline (e.g., 'by the 10th of the month') was found; recipients are told to contact their case manager and verification is required at recertification, but the exact intra-month reporting deadline is not published.

The current FY2026/FY2027 Delaware SNAP E&T State Plan is not yet publicly indexed; verification relied on the FY2025 plan (Wayback), the live DHSS SNAP Alert page, and FY2026 OBBBA secondary sources. Direct WebFetch of the FY2025 plan PDF, USDA FNS DE state-plan page, Ballotpedia, and two USDA FNA waiver pages timed out / returned 429 this pass.
